shared_properties: The storehouse uses a 96x64px footprint that reads as wider and heavier than the longhouse.
Core palette fields remain fixed at primary #111111, background #000000, and accent #FFFFFF.
Large door bands and stacked cargo silhouettes help players connect the building to storage capacity.
differences: This structure communicates resource storage and storm-loss mitigation inside the settlement loop.
Wide roof overhangs and reinforced beams distinguish it from population and military buildings.
Exterior crate silhouettes create a practical worksite feel without cluttering the footprint.

reference_images.label: jarl_of_blizzard_gdd
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the winter survival loop, queue-memory mechanic, building roster, and 1920x1080 Bevy target.

reference_images.label: jarl_of_blizzard_village_scene
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the settlement composition, building spacing, and whiteout layering over the frozen basin.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Static building sprite placed as the main storage structure for resource-capacity feedback and storm mitigation cues.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load world/art_000012.png during building boot and instantiate it as a static sprite on warehouse tiles.
runtime_notes: Place the sprite on a two-tile-wide footprint while keeping the center point aligned with the world grid.
Use static art rather than a sheet because storage readability matters more than ambient motion here.
Keep cargo silhouettes near the lower third so UI overlays can sit above the roofline if needed.

context.usage: Wide storage building used to signal resource capacity, storm protection, and winter stockpile planning.

shape.silhouette: Wide timber storehouse with a heavy roof, broad cargo doors, and stacked crate silhouettes.

acceptance_criteria: PNG canvas is exactly 96x64px with the roof fully contained inside the bounds.
The building reads wider and heavier than art_000011 when both are displayed at 1x scale.
Door bands and cargo silhouettes remain readable without blending into nearby snow tiles.
